fact: July 30 is exactly 211 days into the year, which means there are exactly 154 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak summer heat time - the sun stays up for about 14-15 hours in most places, making it perfect for super long outdoor adventures.
Cicadas are screaming their loudest around July 30 in many parts of North America - some can buzz as loud as a motorcycle!
Fireflies seem extra magical around July 30 because it falls during their peak flashing season when they're trying to find mates by blinking their butts.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, July 30 is smack in the middle of winter - penguins are huddling together while kids in America are swimming!
Sunflowers are typically turning into giant yellow dinner plates around this date - they can grow taller than a basketball player and their heads can weigh 10 pounds!
The zodiac sign switches from Cancer to Leo around July 30, which astrologers say means the sky energy goes from crabby and moody to bold and dramatic.
Mosquitoes are usually at their most annoying peak around July 30 in many places because the hot, humid weather makes them breed like crazy in puddles and ponds.
In northern places like Alaska, the sun barely sets around July 30 - it just dips down and pops right back up like a yo-yo, giving kids almost 20 hours of daylight to play outside.
Corn stalks are typically shooting up like green rockets around this time - they can grow 2-3 inches taller every single day in July heat!
Beach sand gets so scorching hot around July 30 that it can literally cook an egg - temperatures can reach 140°F, which is hot enough to burn bare feet in seconds.
Bats are having midnight snack parties around this date because there are more flying insects buzzing around than any other time of year - a single bat can gobble up 1,000 mosquitoes in one hour!
